tasks.title: Add node:test harness to package.json scripts
tasks.description: Phase 1 (Test harness setup). Add "test": "node --test" to the scripts block in package.json. Confirm node --test discovers files matching test/*.test.js. Do NOT add any new dependency (preserve the zero-dependency invariant). Do NOT add test/ to the published files array (files must stay bin/src/README/LICENSE) so tests are not shipped to npm. Acceptance: running `npm test` invokes `node --test`; package.json files array unchanged; no entries added to dependencies or devDependencies.

tasks.title: Export parseFlags from src/cli.js (additive)
tasks.description: Phase 2 (Enable testability). Add parseFlags to module.exports in src/cli.js so it can be unit-tested. This is a one-line additive change only: the existing run export must remain unchanged, and parseFlags logic must not be refactored. Acceptance: require('../src/cli').parseFlags is a function; require('../src/cli').run is still exported; no behavioral change to run.

tasks.title: Write test/util.test.js for pure helpers
tasks.description: Phase 3 (Core pure-function tests). Create test/util.test.js using node:test and node:assert. Cover src/util.js: checkResp happy path and error path (assert error carries .status and .body), readData, and maybeJson (valid JSON string parsed, non-JSON returned as-is/handled). Tests must be deterministic and avoid network/fs/process.exit. Acceptance: `node --test test/util.test.js` passes green; covers checkResp success + error, readData, maybeJson branches.

tasks.title: Write test/cli.test.js for parseFlags branching
tasks.description: Phase 3 (Core pure-function tests). Create test/cli.test.js using node:test and node:assert covering parseFlags from src/cli.js. Cover branches: bare -- terminator, --api-key value and --api-key=value, --page, --data, generic --k v and --k=v, and positional arguments. Tests must be deterministic and avoid process.exit paths. Acceptance: `node --test test/cli.test.js` passes green; all listed parseFlags branches asserted.

tasks.title: Wire config and docs to node --test
tasks.description: Phase 4 (Config/doc wiring). Update .jonggrang/jonggrang.json testing block: set framework to node:test (or node-test) and command to "node --test". Update the AGENTS.md Testing section to document running tests via `node --test` with the test/ mirror layout. Align CLAUDE.md test-command references and the pre_commit hook in jonggrang.json that currently echoes 'no test command configured' so it runs the real test command. Acceptance: jonggrang.json testing.command is `node --test`; AGENTS.md Testing section reflects node:test; CLAUDE.md and pre_commit no longer claim no test command configured.

tasks.title: Validate suite and commit atomically
tasks.description: Phase 5 (Validate & commit). Run `node --test` and confirm the full suite is green and deterministic (run twice to check for flake). Make a single atomic commit with a test(...) prefix covering the harness, exports, tests, and doc/config wiring. Append learnings to .jonggrang/progress.txt. Acceptance: node --test exits 0 on repeated runs; one commit prefixed test(...); progress.txt updated with learnings.

tasks.title: (Optional follow-up) Extend tests to I/O seams
tasks.description: Phase 6 (Optional / follow-up). Extend coverage toward the 80% aspiration by testing I/O-bound modules safely: config with a temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME (os.tmpdir), ui with process.stdout.write capture, and representative commands by stubbing api.request. Set env (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, MAYAR_API_URL) before require and use delete require.cache where module-load-time reads matter; never touch the real ~/.config/mayar or api.mayar.id. Avoid or stub process.exit paths (cli.run, ui.askSecret). This is optional and non-blocking. Acceptance: new tests green and deterministic; no real fs/network side effects.
